coreIssue: This 1:1 is likely about whether a junior Product Designer three months in feels clear, supported, and able to navigate changing requirements without withdrawing.
openingQuestion: What feels hardest to get right right now when priorities shift and you're still trying to understand what good looks like here?
listenFor: whether he names a specific recent project where changing requirements made his work harder
whether if they ask for help, he can point to who he goes to and when
whether he pauses or stays vague when asked what doing well as a junior Product Designer looks like
avoid: do not turn this exploratory 1:1 into a correction or reassurance monologue before he gives examples
do not assume a confidence issue is personal rather than testing where team setup or expectation-setting is unclear
goodOutcome: You and Kenji have agreed what good performance looks like for a junior Product Designer over the next few weeks and one concrete point where he should ask for help earlier.
suggestedAction: Before the 1:1, pick one recent example of shifting requirements that affected Kenji's work so you can ground the conversation without making it feel like a performance review.
